items.title: Phillip Guston, Lush and Forlorn
items.summary: Also, the Guggenheim Museum union authorizes a strike and new findings on human DNA in cave art.

items.title: Philip Guston’s Lines of Poetry
items.summary: A show of his works from 1964–1978 focuses on the artist’s transformation and on his wife, poet Musa McKim, as a principal supporter and source of inspiration.

items.title: Human DNA in Cave Paintings Can Survive For Millennia, Study Finds
items.summary: The discovery points to the future potential of archaeogenetics in recovering the identities of ancient artists from thousands of years ago.

items.title: The Ballroom Icon Who Paved a New Way
items.summary: Amid all of the kitty-kat meow of today’s Vogue Fem performers, Andre Mizrahi Clark has the stern, calm energy of a lion tipping on its toes.
